channel.title: SEA Food & Agriculture Digest
channel.description: Food and agriculture news from across Southeast Asia — smallholders, cooperatives, corporates, and the regional protein debate.

item.title: Mekong Delta rice cooperative federation secures legal standing in Vietnam reform
item.description: A Vietnamese legal amendment recognising multi-village cooperative federations has cleared its final reading, unlocking shared storage and pooled marketing for around 2,400 rice smallholders across An Giang and Dong Thap. Organisers framed the change as a cornerstone of a just and regenerative transition for the delta, arguing that smallholder equity — not yield alone — is what has been missing from Mekong rice policy.

item.title: Indonesian palm smallholders pilot agroecological intercropping on 1,800 hectares
item.description: A consortium of smallholder unions in North Sumatra has begun a 1,800-hectare intercropping trial layering cocoa, bananas, and native trees into ageing palm blocks. The pilot is framed explicitly as agroecological rather than "sustainable palm", and organisers say it is an attempt to move beyond certification-only models toward smallholder-centred systems change.

item.title: Olam Group expands Thai rice sourcing under regenerative contract terms
item.description: Olam Group has extended its regenerative sourcing contract framework to an additional 6,200 rice farmers across central Thailand, adding soil-health measurement and water-use thresholds to procurement criteria. The company says this brings roughly a third of its Thai rice volume under the new terms, with a target of two thirds by 2028.

item.title: Singapore cultivated-meat startup closes $42 million Series B
item.description: A Singapore-based cultivated chicken startup has closed a $42 million Series B led by a mix of Asian family offices and a European climate fund. Executives pitched the round as proof that alternative proteins remain the protein transition for Asia, pointing to Singapore's permissive regulator as a springboard for regional rollout.

item.title: Philippines cocoa cooperative scales direct-to-chocolatier model across Mindanao
item.description: A cooperative of 340 cocoa farmers in Davao has signed direct-purchase agreements with five artisan chocolate makers in Manila and Cebu, bypassing traditional middlemen. Organisers describe the model as a smallholder-centred alternative to commodity export chains, with members retaining roughly 40 per cent more of the final price than in previous seasons.

item.title: Nestle SEA lifts regenerative sourcing target to 50 per cent by 2030
item.description: Nestle's Southeast Asia unit has revised its regenerative sourcing target upward, committing to source half of its regional agricultural volume under regenerative-agriculture terms by 2030. The announcement names dairy in Malaysia, coffee in Vietnam, and coconut in the Philippines as priority supply sheds, with independent verification handled by a third-party auditor.

item.title: Cambodian inland fisheries cooperatives form national federation
item.description: Twenty-three community fisheries cooperatives around the Tonle Sap have consolidated into a national federation with shared ice-making, cold-chain, and legal-defence capacity. Federation leaders say the structure is designed to give smallholder fishers real bargaining power in the face of industrial trawling encroachment and to channel system finance toward locally governed infrastructure.

item.title: Singapore Food Agency clears second lab-grown seafood product for retail sale
item.description: The Singapore Food Agency has granted retail clearance to a cultivated grouper product developed by a local biotech, the second lab-grown seafood approval in the country. The company is marketing the product as climate-smart protein, with a planned supermarket pilot in two Singapore chains later this quarter.

item.title: Wilmar International publishes first agroecology transition roadmap
item.description: Wilmar International has released a 32-page roadmap laying out how the agri-business intends to shift a portion of its upstream supply toward agroecological production by 2032. The document is notable for using the word "agroecology" rather than the group's prior "sustainable agriculture" framing, and for setting explicit smallholder-equity targets alongside hectare-based metrics.

item.title: Malaysian durian smallholders win organic certification across 900 hectares
item.description: A group of 180 durian growers in Pahang has completed organic certification on around 900 hectares, unlocking premium export channels into Japan and South Korea. The cooperative says certification was the entry ticket, but the longer-term aim is a shift to agroecological orchard management, not a single-label play.

item.title: Myanmar rice exporter shifts to cooperative-first procurement after quality scandal
item.description: A mid-sized Myanmar rice exporter has restructured its procurement to purchase primarily through registered farmer cooperatives following a quality-control scandal last year. Management framed the change as a reluctant but necessary concession that cooperative-owned structures deliver more consistent traceability than fragmented middleman networks.

item.title: Unilever SEA publishes 2025 palm oil NDPE audit with partial gaps
item.description: Unilever's Southeast Asia operation has released audit results covering its 2025 palm oil volumes, reporting 94 per cent NDPE compliance and flagging three supplier groups for remediation. The report does not use agroecological framing but does acknowledge that certification alone has not closed deforestation leakage in Sumatra and Kalimantan.

item.title: GFI APAC forecasts 18 per cent alt-protein revenue growth in Asia for 2026
item.description: The Good Food Institute's APAC arm has published its 2026 market outlook, forecasting 18 per cent year-on-year revenue growth for alternative proteins across the region. The report positions alternative proteins as the central lever of Asia's protein transition and calls for accelerated government support in Thailand, Indonesia, and Vietnam.

item.title: Lao coffee cooperative secures ADB blended-finance facility for agroforestry transition
item.description: The Asian Development Bank has closed a $14 million blended-finance facility with a Bolaven Plateau coffee cooperative, financing a shade-tree and intercropping conversion across 3,100 hectares. The facility is structured to put long-duration concessional capital at the cooperative level rather than at a corporate off-taker — an architecture ADB staff described as closer to "system finance" than conventional supply-chain lending.

item.title: Thai shrimp farmers trial regenerative pond design in Chanthaburi
item.description: A small cohort of shrimp farmers in Chanthaburi has begun trialling a mangrove-integrated pond design intended to cut feed input while restoring adjacent coastal habitat. The pilot involves 42 farm sites and is being documented by a Thai research institute; early results on water quality are described as cautiously promising.

item.title: Vietnam's Ministry of Agriculture opens public consultation on agroecology framework
item.description: Vietnam's Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development has opened a six-week public consultation on a proposed national agroecology framework covering rice, coffee, and aquaculture. The draft uses agroecological language throughout and explicitly names smallholder-centred finance as one of four pillars, a significant shift from prior "sustainable agriculture" framings.

item.title: Singapore supermarket chain expands plant-based shelf space by 30 per cent
item.description: A major Singapore supermarket chain has announced a 30 per cent expansion of its plant-based protein shelf space across 74 stores, positioning the move as part of a climate-smart protein strategy. The expansion is supported by a marketing campaign foregrounding convenience and taste rather than farming-system change.

item.title: Indonesian cocoa farmer app reaches 60,000 users, adds price-discovery tools
item.description: An Indonesian agritech serving cocoa smallholders has passed 60,000 active users and rolled out a price-discovery feature allowing farmers to compare local trader offers. The company frames the service in neutral productivity terms, though independent observers note the price-discovery feature measurably strengthens farmer bargaining position in Sulawesi trading hubs.
